headline: Disability Rights Law With No Budget Behind It
context: The Persons with Disabilities Act 2008 guarantees access to education, employment, and public facilities but includes no enforcement mechanism or dedicated funding.

cards.big: The OKU Act guarantees rights. It funds none of them.
cards.sub: No penalty for non-compliance. No budget line for implementation.

cards.big: 1% employment quota for OKU in the public sector. Actual rate: 0.3%.
cards.sub: The quota has no enforcement mechanism. Ministries self-report.

cards.big: Only 2% of public buildings meet accessibility standards.
cards.sub: UBBL accessibility requirements added in 1991. Compliance auditing: voluntary.

cards.big: OKU welfare allocation: RM450 per month per registered individual.
cards.sub: B40 poverty line: RM2,589. The allowance covers 17% of the poverty threshold.

cards.big: A right without a remedy is a brochure.
cards.sub: The OKU Act reads like legislation. It functions like an aspiration.

cards.big: Attach budgets to mandates. Attach penalties to obligations. Until then, the OKU Act protects nothing except the government's reputation.
